svg.text: Radio Oracle — Jetson Orin Nano Wiring
Common-anode RGB LED · Momentary Action Button · Power Toggle · Volume Pot — buttons + pot read via ADS1115
Pin numbers are BCM (matches config/settings.py); header position numbers shown in parentheses

g.text: color encodes mode · firmware drives active-LOW
long press (≥ 1 s): toggle Librarian mode
closed = device on (radio active) · open = standby
A0 ← pot · A1 ← button · A2 ← power
• 1 × 8 mm common-anode RGB LED (4-lead) + 3 × 330 Ω resistor (¼ W)
• 1 × momentary push-button — short = next, long ≥ 1 s = Librarian mode
• 1 × SPST toggle switch — closed wakes the device; open puts it in standby
• 1 × 10 kΩ linear potentiometer + 1 × ADS1115 I²C ADC + 2 × 10 kΩ pull-up (R4, R5)
Switches read via U1.A1 / U1.A2 (not GPIO) — Tegra234 GPIO INPUT has a loopback bug on JP 6.2.x.
USB DAC plugs into a Jetson USB-A port (no GPIO wiring) — see "DAC plugs in here" label.
